title: eucalyptus incrassata subsp. angulosa
intro: Eucalyptus incrassata subsp. angulosa, also known as Narrow-leaved Peppermint or Broad-leaved Peppermint Gum, is a species of eucalyptus tree that is native to Western Australia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and pinene, that exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and analgesic properties. The health benefits of Eucalyptus incrassata subsp. angulosa include relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and colds, as well as its potential to reduce pain and inflammation. The plant is often used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and essential oils, which can be used topically or inhaled to harness its medicinal properties.
